schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

identity.canonical_title: Concrete Equations to Implement Right Away: Hat H_rm Rel(t)

theory.canonical_statement: \[ \hat H_{\rm rel}(t) = -\frac{\hbar^2}{2\mu}\nabla_r^2 + V_{\text{rel}}(r) + g\,\delta_\epsilon(r) + V_{\rm drive}(r,t). \]
theory.technical_interpretation: Concrete Equations to Implement Right Away: Hat H_rm Rel(t) is a hamiltonian construction, written hat H_rm rel(t) = -(ħ^2)/(2μ)∇_r^2 + V_rel(r) + g δ_ε(r) + V_rm drive(r,t).. It defines a Hamiltonian that generates the system's time evolution and encodes its energy. It is built from ħ, μ, ε, r, V_, g, defining hat H_rm rel(t). Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
